examples.title: Algorithm: Banker's Safety Algorithm
examples.description: Determines if a system is in a safe state by simulating resource allocation
examples.code:
ALGORITHM BankersSafety
BEGIN
 Initialize Work = Available
 Initialize Finish[i] = false for all i
 
 REPEAT
 found = false
 FOR each process Pi:
 IF Finish[i] == false AND Need[i] <= Work THEN
 Work = Work + Allocation[i]
 Finish[i] = true
 found = true
 UNTIL no more processes can be allocated
 
 IF all Finish[i] == true THEN
 return SAFE
 ELSE
 return UNSAFE
END
examples.explanation:
1. Work vector initialized with available resources
2. Iteratively find processes that can complete with current resources
3. Releases allocated resources back to Work
4. Checks if all processes can finish

examples.title: Implementation: Banker's Algorithm
examples.description: Python implementation of safety algorithm with sample data
examples.explanation:
1. Calculates need matrix dynamically
2. Implements safety check through nested loops
3. Returns True if system is in safe state
4. Sample data shows safe allocation scenario

examples.title: Algorithm: Deadlock Detection using Wait-For Graph
examples.description: Detects deadlocks by finding cycles in wait-for graph
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DetectDeadlockWFG
BEGIN
 Construct wait-for graph from resource dependencies
 FOR each process Pi not visited:
 IF DFS(Pi, path) returns true:
 RETURN 'Deadlock Detected'
 RETURN 'No Deadlock'
END

FUNCTION DFS(process, path)
BEGIN
 ADD process to path
 MARK process as visited
 
 FOR each neighbor Pj in wait-for graph:
 IF Pj in path:
 RETURN true # Cycle detected
 IF not visited AND DFS(Pj, path):
 RETURN true
 
 REMOVE process from path
 RETURN false
END
examples.explanation:
1. Converts resource allocation to wait-for graph
2. Uses DFS to detect cycles in graph
3. Maintains path stack for cycle detection
4. Returns immediately when cycle found

examples.title: Algorithm: Victim Selection for Recovery
examples.description: Selects process with lowest priority & resources for termination
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SelectVictim
BEGIN
 candidates = [processes in deadlock]
 IF empty(candidates):
 RETURN error

 victim = candidates[0]
 min_priority = victim.priority
 min_resources = count_resources(victim)

 FOR process IN candidates[1:]:
 curr_priority = process.priority
 curr_res = count_resources(process)
 
 IF (curr_priority < min_priority) OR 
 (curr_priority == min_priority AND curr_res < min_resources):
 victim = process
 min_priority = curr_priority
 min_resources = curr_res

 RETURN victim
END
examples.explanation:
1. Prioritizes processes with lower priority values
2. Breaks ties using resource count
3. Selects process minimizing recovery cost
4. Works with any deadlocked process subset
